Node.js Basics, Part 7: Debugging with Chrome DevTools

Let's talk about debugging in Node.js. The most common approach is console.log(), but typing console.log() over and over gets tedious — so here's another way to debug Node.js, using breakpoints and Chrome DevTools.
Node.js Basics, Part 6: Auto-Restarting Your App with nodemon

In the past, every time we changed a js file we had to stop and re-run node all.js, which gets old fast. This post introduces a really handy tool — nodemon — that restarts your app automatically whenever you save.
Quick notes on npm install variations so I don't forget — even though I type them all the time.
A note on how to read npm version numbers — for when I forget what the symbols mean.
Node.js Basics, Part 5: Spinning Up a Web Server with createServer

Next we'll learn how to start a web server. Node.js ships with web server functionality built in, so let's see how to create one using the http module's createServer method, and understand request and response along the way.
Node.js Basics, Part 4: Modular Development with require and module.exports

Next up, some notes on modular development in Node.js. Before we get into it, there are a couple of pieces of syntax we need to know — namely require and module.exports — and how to share variables and functions across files.
Node.js Basics, Part 3: The global Object, and How It Differs From window

When we write JavaScript in the browser, we know there's a global object called window. Node.js has its own global object too — but it behaves a little differently, especially once modules come into play.
Node.js Basics, Part 2: Writing JavaScript Right in the REPL
This one's pretty short. It's mostly a note about how you can use Node's REPL mode to write JavaScript straight in the terminal, without creating a file first.
Node.js Basics, Part 1: Install It, Meet the V8 Engine, and Run Hello World

Kicking off another basics series — this one's a set of notes I'm writing to get my own head straight on Node.js concepts and development. In this first post we'll cover what Node.js actually is, the V8 engine behind it, why it's worth using, how to install it, and how to run your very first Hello World.
Drag a Video Onto a `.bat` File to Auto-Generate Subtitles With Python
Following my earlier post on using Python to auto-subtitle videos, I got tired of typing the same command every time. Here's how I built a `.bat` file so you can just drag-and-drop a video onto it.
